when_to_use: big band swing, dancehall energy, head->soli->solo break->shout chorus arc, 115-180 BPM

defaults.feel: 4-beat straight-ahead swing, ride-driven, walking bass pushing slightly ahead of the beat

signature_moves: major line cliche: F -> Fmaj7 -> F7 -> F6
rhythm-changes turnaround: Gm7 -> C7 -> F6 D7 -> Gm7 C7
cadence_options: ii-V-I with a 6th-chord landing: Gm7 -> C7 -> F6
chromatic turnaround: F6 -> F#dim7 -> Gm7 -> G#dim7
big-band tag ending: held dominant chord (vibrato) into a staccato unison hit
avoid: altered/upper-structure extensions everywhere - this era resolves with 6th chords and diminished passing chords, not modern-jazz density (see classic-jazz-genre.md SS5)
a static vamp with no functional resolution - classic jazz harmony moves

hook_archetypes.name: riff-and-answer
hook_archetypes.rhythm: a 2-bar brass riff stated staccato, answered by a 2-bar legato reed phrase
hook_archetypes.contour: brass riff outlines the 6th chord, sax answer descends through the line cliche
hook_archetypes.first_appears: Head A1, brass states it, reeds answer

hook_archetypes.name: solo-break-lick
hook_archetypes.rhythm: an acrobatic unaccompanied phrase filling a 2-4 bar solo break, band silent
hook_archetypes.contour: arpeggiate up through the chord, chromatic enclosure into the landing note
hook_archetypes.first_appears: the top of the Solo section, right after the band cuts out on beat 1

head.note_lengths: clear, mid-length phrases with room to breathe
head.contour: singable, riff-based, outlines the 6th/dominant chords plainly

soli.note_lengths: fast, closely-spaced block-chord runs, precisely together
soli.contour: the whole reed section moves as one voice in four-way-close harmony
soli.placement: tight to the grid - ensemble unity over individual expression

solo-break.note_lengths: dense and virtuosic inside the break, then normal phrase length once the band re-enters
solo-break.contour: an acrobatic unaccompanied run resolving right as the band explodes back in
solo-break.placement: free within the break bars, locked back to the grid the instant the band re-enters

shout-chorus.contour: full-ensemble tutti stabs at maximum dynamic, optional half-step modulation for extra lift
shout-chorus.placement: on the grid, unison ensemble hits

drum_skeleton.low_energy: feathered kick on all 4 beats (barely audible), hi-hat chick on 2 and 4, ride swinging 8ths throughout
drum_skeleton.mid_energy: ride pattern drives harder, occasional rimshot backbeat accents, kick adds light syncopated pickups
drum_skeleton.high_energy: press-rolls and fills into section arrivals, snare accents doubling ensemble hits, full band-locked hits at the shout chorus

arrangement_defaults.layout_rules: stride piano or drum roll-off opens alone; reeds state the head with brass answering; reed section soli in block chords; brass solo opens with an unaccompanied solo break, other horns lay in background riffs under the solo; full ensemble tutti for the shout chorus; tag ending strips back to a held chord plus a final unison hit

anti_boredom_rules: the solo break (band silent, soloist alone for 2-4 bars) must happen at least once before the shout chorus - it is structural, not optional
the shout chorus must be denser and louder than the head - confirm the arc actually builds soli -> solo -> shout
assign horn articulation (fall-off, doit, shake, growl, glissando) deliberately per phrase - never uniform legato
the riff-and-answer hook must recur varied by section/register, never copy-pasted identically
velocity is never flat within a bar - ghost/background riffs stay low, ensemble hits and shout-chorus stabs sit high
